mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive sentiment for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth typically correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and reduces the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank rate hikes historically precede market corrections.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following a triggered FEDFUNDS increase, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate strong consumer confidence and spending power, driving corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of periods where both retail sales growth and low unemployment conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts (HOUST) during an early-cycle recovery period signals a rebound in construction activity and broader economic confidence, which translates to positive short-term equity market sentiment over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where HOUST shows a significant month-over-month increase during an early recovery, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling a healthy supply side of the economy that supports corporate earnings and long-term equity appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of manufacturing health.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 60-day returns turns negative or becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, indicating weakening consumer spending and corporate profits, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes or accompanies recessions.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 35% of periods following a triggered increase in UNRATE, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a stagflationary environment, where corporate margins are squeezed and economic uncertainty rises, leading to negative equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND GDPC1 annual growth < 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary periods are historically challenging for equities.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods when both high inflation and low GDP growth conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) during a period of consumer spending contraction indicates weakening demand, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and leads to short-term negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending drives a large portion of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where RSAFS shows a significant month-over-month decline during a contraction, the hypothesis is falsified.
